configurations.name: with_skill
configurations.description: Using the EAROS rubric creator skill

configurations.name: without_skill
configurations.description: Baseline — no skill, general knowledge only

analyst_observations: The skill achieves 100% EAROS schema conformance across all 3 test cases — the baseline achieves ~11%.
Baseline outputs use completely different YAML structures (custom field names, different scales, no inheritance) making them incompatible with the EAROS ecosystem.
The only non-trivial baseline passes are 'valid YAML' and 'criteria count <= 12' (vacuously true when the grader finds 0 criteria in the expected structure).
The skill adds ~95 seconds and ~18.5k tokens of overhead — this is the cost of reading reference docs and following the structured interview.

Two of three with-skill outputs had YAML quoting issues (unquoted strings containing double quotes in list items). This is a known LLM YAML generation weakness. The skill should add explicit guidance about quoting strings with special characters.
All with-skill outputs correctly selected the appropriate rubric type (profile vs overlay) and design method (A for API, D for regulatory, C for migration).
